excerpt: So how do fillers work so dermal fillers are made or any product that adds volume to your skin and even say line can be dermal filler and on the other end of the spectrum using your own fat can be considered a dermal filler in the market we're currently working in most dermal fil…

segments.text: so how do fillers work so dermal fillers are made or any product that adds volume to your skin and even say line can be dermal filler and on the other end of the spectrum using your own fat can be considered a dermal filler in the market we're currently working in most dermal fillers are hyaluronic acid dermal fillers with a few exceptions the great thing about hyaluronic acid dermal fillers is that they are a copy of something that's already in your skin so dermal fillers work by emulating what's already in the skin and they've varied some of the properties as well so you can create a bit more shape or volume or use it to treat a crease and or create definition these are all properties of dermal fillers and that's how they work by creating shape so you can make someone more beautiful by enhancing the angles on their face you can also make them more beautiful by replacing volume so they replace that lost fat pads to create a more harmonious and youthful look you can also use them to create detail so you can hunt a Vermilion border for example you can

also use them to remove shadows adding a little bit of volume replacing fat pads and structure so they're essentially just a gel and they're different versions of this gel that either enabler to make the surface of the skin smoother or to enable the structure underneath to be more prominent and so extremely versatile you can solve all manner of categories of problems with them and they are certainly not just about filling and their shape and contour and detail and a lot of artistry that you can get out of using a good dermal filler product [Music]

transcript: so how do fillers work so dermal fillers are made or any product that adds volume to your skin and even say line can be dermal filler and on the other end of the spectrum using your own fat can be considered a dermal filler in the market we're currently working in most dermal fillers are hyaluronic acid dermal fillers with a few exceptions the great thing about hyaluronic acid dermal fillers is that they are a copy of something that's already in your skin so dermal fillers work by emulating what's already in the skin and they've varied some of the properties as well so you can create a bit more shape or volume or use it to treat a crease and or create definition these are all properties of dermal fillers and that's how they work by creating shape so you can make someone more beautiful by enhancing the angles on their face you can also make them more beautiful by replacing volume so they replace that lost fat pads to create a more harmonious and youthful look you can also use them to create detail so you can hunt a Vermilion border for example you can also use them to remove shadows adding a little bit of volume replacing fat pads and structure so they're essentially just a gel and they're different versions of this gel that either enabler to make the surface of the skin smoother or to enable the structure underneath to be more prominent and so extremely versatile you can solve all manner of categories of problems with them and they are certainly not just about filling and their shape and contour and detail and a lot of artistry that you can get out of using a good dermal filler product [Music]
